content:
URGENT EXPO EXECUTION TASK — FIX THE DEAD TUNNEL AND ANDROID STARTUP CRASH END TO END

The current Expo URL is:

exp://neat-bullfrog-49.loca.lt

Independent external verification currently returns:

HTTPS_TUNNEL_STATUS=503 Service Unavailable

The existing tunnel is dead, expired, disconnected, or no longer forwarding to Metro.

Do not reuse this URL.
Do not return another audit-only response.
Create a fresh working Expo connection, capture the real crash if one remains, fix it, and prove the app opens on Android.

==================================================
1. VERIFY THE CURRENT TUNNEL FAILURE
==================================================

Test:

https://neat-bullfrog-49.loca.lt

Return:

OLD_TUNNEL_HTTP_STATUS=
OLD_TUNNEL_ACTIVE=YES/NO
OLD_TUNNEL_UPSTREAM_CONNECTED=YES/NO

Do not claim Expo is running while this endpoint returns 503.

==================================================
2. VERIFY THE METRO PROCESS
==================================================

From the actual project workspace verify:

- Expo CLI process is running
- Metro process is running
- Metro port
- Process PID
- Workspace path
- Repository
- Branch
- Current commit
- No duplicate Metro process
- No stale tunnel process

Return:

PROJECT_PATH=
REPOSITORY=
BRANCH=
COMMIT_SHA=
METRO_RUNNING=YES/NO
METRO_PORT=
METRO_PID=
DUPLICATE_METRO_PROCESSES=

==================================================
3. STOP ALL STALE PROCESSES
==================================================

Stop:

- Old Expo CLI processes
- Old Metro processes
- Old LocalTunnel processes
- Old ngrok processes
- Processes holding Metro ports
- Duplicate packagers

Verify the intended port is free before restarting.

Return:

STALE_PROCESSES_STOPPED=
METRO_PORT_FREE=YES/NO

==================================================
4. CLEAR DEVELOPMENT CACHES
==================================================

Clear:

- Metro cache
- Expo cache
- .expo local state
- Watchman cache if available
- Temporary bundle cache
- Stale route cache

Then reinstall only if dependency integrity requires it.

Use the equivalent of:

npx expo start --clear

Do not delete application source code or production data.

==================================================
5. VALIDATE EXPO CONFIGURATION
==================================================

Run Expo diagnostics and verify:

- expo package version
- Expo SDK version
- React Native version
- Expo Router version
- package compatibility
- app.json/app.config configuration
- entry point
- scheme
- Android package name
- plugins
- runtime version
- updates configuration

Run dependency validation using the appropriate Expo diagnostic command.

Return:

EXPO_SDK=
EXPO_CLI=
REACT_NATIVE_VERSION=
EXPO_ROUTER_VERSION=
DEPENDENCY_CHECK=PASS/FAIL
CONFIG_CHECK=PASS/FAIL
INCOMPATIBLE_PACKAGES=

==================================================
6. DETERMINE WHETHER EXPO GO IS SUPPORTED
==================================================

Search every dependency and plugin for custom native code.

Identify:

- Packages unsupported by Expo Go
- Config plugins requiring prebuild
- Native modules requiring a development build
- Custom Android native code
- Packages unavailable in the installed Expo Go SDK

Return:

EXPO_GO_COMPATIBLE=YES/NO
UNSUPPORTED_NATIVE_MODULES=
DEVELOPMENT_BUILD_REQUIRED=YES/NO

If a development build is required, do not continue pretending Expo Go can run the full application.

==================================================
7. START METRO LOCALLY FIRST
==================================================

Start Metro without a tunnel.

Verify:

- Local Metro root responds
- Manifest endpoint responds
- JavaScript bundle endpoint responds
- No immediate terminal exception
- QR is generated from the active process

Return:

LOCAL_METRO_HTTP_STATUS=
MANIFEST_HTTP_STATUS=
BUNDLE_HTTP_STATUS=
LOCAL_START_STATUS=

==================================================
8. START A NEW TUNNEL
==================================================

Create a brand-new tunnel using:

npx expo start --tunnel --clear

Do not reuse neat-bullfrog-49.loca.lt.

Capture:

NEW_EXPO_URL=
NEW_HTTPS_TUNNEL_URL=
TUNNEL_PROVIDER=
TUNNEL_STARTED_AT=

==================================================
9. VERIFY THE NEW TUNNEL BEFORE SHARING IT
==================================================

Test the new tunnel externally.

Required:

- HTTP must not be 503
- Manifest must be reachable
- Bundle must be reachable
- Tunnel must forward to the active Metro port

Return:

NEW_TUNNEL_HTTP_STATUS=
NEW_TUNNEL_MANIFEST_STATUS=
NEW_TUNNEL_BUNDLE_STATUS=
NEW_TUNNEL_ACTIVE=YES/NO

Do not share the QR or exp:// URL until all checks pass.

==================================================
10. OPEN THE URL CORRECTLY IN EXPO GO
==================================================

On Android:

- Open Expo Go directly
- Use Enter URL manually or Expo Go QR scanner
- Enter the new exp:// URL
- Do not open the api.qrserver.com image in Chrome
- Do not use the old tunnel
- Confirm Expo Go receives the manifest

Return:

EXPO_GO_OPENED=YES/NO
MANIFEST_RECEIVED=YES/NO
BUNDLE_DOWNLOAD_STARTED=YES/NO

==================================================
11. CAPTURE THE REAL STARTUP ERROR
==================================================

If the app still crashes after the tunnel is working, capture:

- Expo Go error screen
- Metro terminal stack trace
- JavaScript exception
- File
- Line
- Function
- Android logcat fatal exception
- Native module error
- Missing environment variable
- Router error
- Asset failure

Use:

- Metro terminal logs
- React Native DevTools
- Android system logs / logcat

Return:

CRASH_REPRODUCED=YES/NO
ERROR_MESSAGE=
ERROR_FILE=
ERROR_LINE=
ERROR_FUNCTION=
ANDROID_FATAL_EXCEPTION=
TRACE_ID=

Do not return “crashed” without the actual stack trace.

==================================================
12. AUDIT STARTUP IMPORTS
==================================================

Audit all code executed before the first screen:

- root layout
- app entry
- Expo Router entry
- providers
- authentication provider
- Supabase initialization
- API client
- environment loader
- secure storage
- notification setup
- fonts
- splash screen
- analytics
- owner role loader
- project/media loader

Find any import or initialization that throws synchronously.

==================================================
13. AUDIT ENVIRONMENT VARIABLES
==================================================

Verify required client-safe variables exist in the Expo runtime.

Do not expose secret values.

Check:

- EXPO_PUBLIC_* variables
- Supabase public URL
- Supabase anon key
- API base URL
- WebSocket URL
- environment mode
- feature flags

Never bundle:

- Service role key
- GitHub token
- Render key
- Owner secrets

Return:

REQUIRED_PUBLIC_ENV_PRESENT=YES/NO
MISSING_PUBLIC_ENV_NAMES=
SERVER_SECRETS_EXPOSED=YES/NO

==================================================
14. AUDIT EXPO ROUTER
==================================================

Verify:

- Root `app/_layout.tsx` exists
- Initial route exists
- No duplicate routes
- No invalid redirect loop
- No route points to undefined screen
- No browser-only API executes on Android
- No route performs infinite authentication redirects
- Error boundary exists

Return:

ROUTER_QA=PASS/FAIL
INITIAL_ROUTE=
REDIRECT_LOOP_DETECTED=YES/NO

==================================================
15. AUDIT NATIVE-MODULE COMPATIBILITY
==================================================

For every native-dependent package verify Expo Go support.

Typical high-risk categories:

- custom camera modules
- biometric modules
- payment SDKs
- custom video codecs
- background services
- native database packages
- custom notification packages
- Bluetooth/NFC
- proprietary authentication SDKs
- unsupported file-system packages

If unsupported:

- Guard the import in Expo Go, or
- Replace it with an Expo-compatible module, or
- Build a custom development client

Do not let an unsupported native import crash startup.

==================================================
16. AUDIT ASSETS
==================================================

Verify:

- Images exist
- Fonts exist
- File-name case matches
- No broken dynamic require()
- No unsupported asset format
- Asset paths are valid
- Splash and icon assets exist
- No excessively large startup asset blocks launch

Return:

ASSET_QA=PASS/FAIL
BROKEN_ASSETS=

==================================================
17. FIX THE FIRST REAL CRASH
==================================================

Fix the exact highest-priority error from the captured stack trace.

Then:

- Restart Metro
- Reload Expo Go
- Confirm the first screen renders
- Continue until there are no startup crashes

Do not hide errors with an empty catch block.

==================================================
18. ADD A STARTUP ERROR BOUNDARY
==================================================

Add a production-safe root error boundary that shows:

- Error ID
- Retry button
- Reload app button
- Safe diagnostic summary
- Environment
- App version
- Commit/version marker

Do not expose credentials or private data.

==================================================
19. ADD STARTUP HEALTH DIAGNOSTICS
==================================================

Record safe startup phases:

- APP_ENTRY_STARTED
- CONFIG_LOADED
- PROVIDERS_MOUNTED
- AUTH_INITIALIZED
- API_INITIALIZED
- ROUTER_READY
- FIRST_SCREEN_RENDERED

If startup fails, record the last successful phase and trace ID.

==================================================
20. TEST NETWORK CONDITIONS
==================================================

Test:

- Wi-Fi
- Cellular
- VPN off
- Private DNS off
- Fresh tunnel
- Offline then reconnect
- Slow connection
- Tunnel restart

The app must show an error state instead of closing.

==================================================
21. TEST ANDROID EXPO GO
==================================================

Verify:

- Expo Go opens
- Manifest downloads
- Bundle downloads
- Splash completes
- First screen renders
- Login screen renders
- Home screen renders
- Hardware back works
- App survives background/foreground
- Reload works
- No Chrome redirect
- No raw QR page
- No startup crash

==================================================
22. BUILD A DEVELOPMENT CLIENT IF REQUIRED
==================================================

If EXPO_GO_COMPATIBLE=NO:

- Create an Android development build
- Include all required native modules
- Install it on the test Android device
- Connect it to Metro
- Test the complete IVX application there

Return:

ANDROID_DEVELOPMENT_BUILD_ID=
DEVELOPMENT_CLIENT_INSTALLED=YES/NO
DEVELOPMENT_CLIENT_QA=PASS/FAIL

Do not treat Expo Go limitations as application failures.

==================================================
23. COMMIT AND PUSH THE FIX
==================================================

After the startup issue is fixed:

- Run tests
- Run typecheck
- Run Expo export/build validation
- Secret scan
- Commit
- Push to GitHub main

Return:

TESTS_PASSED=
TESTS_FAILED=
TYPECHECK=
EXPORT_OR_BUILD_STATUS=
SECRET_SCAN=
COMMIT_SHA=
PUSH_STATUS=

==================================================
24. PROVIDE A WORKING CONNECTION
==================================================

Return only a newly verified working URL:

NEW_EXPO_URL=
NEW_TUNNEL_HTTP_STATUS=
GENERATED_AT=
TESTED_ON_ANDROID=YES/NO

Do not return the old URL:

exp://neat-bullfrog-49.loca.lt

==================================================
25. FINAL EVIDENCE
==================================================

Return exactly:

OLD_EXPO_URL=exp://neat-bullfrog-49.loca.lt
OLD_TUNNEL_HTTP_STATUS=
ROOT_CAUSE=
METRO_RUNNING=
METRO_PORT=
EXPO_SDK=
EXPO_GO_COMPATIBLE=
DEVELOPMENT_BUILD_REQUIRED=
NEW_EXPO_URL=
NEW_TUNNEL_HTTP_STATUS=
MANIFEST_HTTP_STATUS=
BUNDLE_HTTP_STATUS=
CRASH_REPRODUCED=
ERROR_MESSAGE=
ERROR_FILE=
ERROR_LINE=
ROUTER_QA=
ENV_QA=
ASSET_QA=
ANDROID_EXPO_GO_QA=
ANDROID_DEVELOPMENT_BUILD_QA=
FIRST_SCREEN_RENDERED=
COMMIT_SHA=
PUSH_STATUS=
FINAL_STATUS=VERIFIED/UNVERIFIED

Do not report VERIFIED unless the new URL was opened successfully on a real Android device and the IVX first screen rendered without crashing.
